title: Backend capability descriptor (Contract 4)
description: Declares what an inference engine EXPOSES: features, limits, and observation points. Normative. Boundary rule (hard program rule 4): this descriptor describes engine-owned facts; it MUST NOT carry scheduling directives for the gateway (continuous batching, per-token scheduling, KV/prefix-cache internals, and GPU placement are engine-owned — the gateway consumes these fields as feature gates and probe configuration only). Engine metric names and endpoint shapes are volatile: every descriptor carries a dated provenance block, and metric names are MAPPED per descriptor, never hardcoded by consumers (as of 2026-07 — re-verify at use time).

commit.description: Build commit; null only when genuinely unavailable.

protocol.description: Wire framing of the engine's streaming responses.

usage_in_stream.description: Whether the engine can deliver token usage inside the stream (Contract 1 stream_options.include_usage). If false, the gateway must settle usage from its own token accounting.

mechanism.description: How usage arrives (e.g. final chunk when stream_options.include_usage=true).

cancellation.description: How the engine learns a request is canceled and how release of its resources can be OBSERVED. Contract 1 makes observable release part of cancellation conformance; a descriptor without release observability is invalid.

mechanism.description: http-connection-close: closing the upstream request body/connection aborts generation. explicit-abort-endpoint: a separate abort call is required. none: engine runs to completion regardless (cancellation non-conformant).

propagation_note.description: Engine-specific behavior detail (e.g. abort detected at next scheduling step).

signals.description: Concrete signals that confirm the engine released the request's resources after cancel (canonical metric keys from metrics.name_mapping, or log patterns). MUST be non-empty when observable=true.

metrics.description: Engine metrics exposure. NORMATIVE: consumers MUST resolve engine metric names through name_mapping, never hardcode them — names vary by engine and engine version (e.g. vLLM waiting/KV-usage gauges; as of 2026-07, re-verify at use time).

endpoint.description: Path of the engine's metrics endpoint (e.g. /metrics); null when the engine exposes none.

as_of.description: Date the mapping was last verified against a running engine of the stated version.

verified_against_version.description: Exact engine version the mapping was verified against.

entries.description: Canonical signal key -> engine-native metric name (null = engine has no equivalent). Canonical keys are stable contract surface; engine names are per-descriptor data.

id.description: Tokenizer identity (e.g. HF repo id, gguf-embedded, mock scheme). Token counts are comparable only under the same tokenizer.

context_limit_tokens.description: Maximum context (input + output) the engine accepts for the served model as configured.

concurrency_hints.description: HINTS for admission sizing, not scheduling directives: the gateway may cap in-flight requests using these, but MUST NOT attempt to batch or schedule tokens itself.

max_concurrent_requests.description: Engine-configured concurrent request ceiling; null when the engine advertises none.

observable.description: Whether prefix-cache effectiveness can be observed externally (metrics/logs), as opposed to only inferred from latency.

signals.description: Canonical metric keys or log patterns evidencing cache behavior. MUST be non-empty when observable=true.

active.description: Quantization active for the served model (e.g. awq, fp8, gguf-q4_k_m); null = none.

methods_supported.description: Methods this engine build can load. May be empty.

supported.description: Whether the engine accepts a per-request priority signal.

mechanism.description: How priority is conveyed (engine-owned scheduling; the gateway only forwards the signal).

provenance.description: How and when this descriptor's facts were established. Ecosystem facts here are volatile (as of 2026-07 — re-verify at use time).

method.description: probed: verified against a running engine; declared: asserted by the operator; source-reported: taken from engine docs/source.
